channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Colossal Biosciences breeds controversy while trying to revive mammoths
item.description: A Texas biotech company is trying to bring mammoths and other extinct creatures back to life. The science is as intriguing as the ethical questions ar...

item.title: A Biden-era rule sought to stabilize child care. Why Trump wants it gone
item.description: The Trump administration has proposed repealing a Biden-era rule that required states to change how they pay out child care subsidies, citing the pote...

item.title: GOP Rep. Tony Gonzales heads to a runoff in Texas amid a new ethics probe in the House
item.description: Texas Republican Rep. Tony Gonzales has faced increasing pressure from his party to resign or drop out of his race after allegations of an affair with...

item.title: The 2026 Oscar-nominated documentaries are sensitive and transformative
item.description: This year's Oscar-nominated documentaries include a tale of terminal cancer, dispatches from behind bars in Alabama, and stories from Iran and Russia.

item.title: Satellite images show Iran school strike hit more buildings than earlier reported
item.description: The images suggest that precision munitions struck other buildings, including a clinic that was also inside the complex.

item.title: Texan James Talarico becomes a fresh face of Democrats’ midterm hopes after Senate primary win
Officers who killed Austin gunman won't face charges
item.description: Travis County DA Jose Garza said Tuesday that suggestions that he would seek charges in the killing were "intentionally false" and political in nature...

item.title: Senate voting on Trump's war powers after attack on Iran
item.description: Members of Congress are voting Wednesday on a war powers resolution aimed at blocking President Trump from using additional military force against Ira...

item.title: Pentagon identifies four of six US soldiers killed in Iran conflict
item.description: The US soldiers were killed after a drone evaded air defences to hit a command centre in Port Shuaiba, Kuwait, on Sunday.

item.title: Watershed moment as Russia's sporting exile ends
item.description: Sports Editor Dan Roan analyses Russia's controversial return to global sporting action at the Winter Paralympics and what it might mean for other spo...

item.title: Gov. Tim Walz tells a House panel the Trump immigration crackdown hampered Minnesota’s fraud fight
Iran postpones Khamenei funeral as US-Israeli bombardment continues
item.description: An official says more preparations are needed for the three-day ceremony in Tehran, where the supreme leader will lie in state.

item.title: Russia blames Ukrainian naval drones as tanker sinks in Mediterranean
item.description: The Arctic Metagaz went down between Libya and Malta after it was hit by explosions and a fire, Libyan officials say.

item.title: Hegseth says U.S. "just getting started" as Iran war spreads
item.description: The U.S. is "accelerating, not decelerating" war on Iran, Hegseth says, as strikes intensify in the region and reach 1,000 miles away.

item.title: US soldiers who died in the Iran war remembered for their service and devotion to their families
item.description: Four of the six U.S. soldiers killed in the Iran war have been identified by the Pentagon as members of the Army Reserve.

item.title: 'Every day feels like a month': Iranians describe life under 'constant' US-Israeli strikes
item.description: Iranians are coping with daily strikes, internet blackouts, and security crackdowns - all while trying to stay in touch with loved ones.

item.title: State Department spokesperson on what Americans in the Middle East should do for evacuations
item.description: Some Americans in the Middle East are voicing frustration with what they say is a lack of help from the U.S. government as the State Department urges ...

item.title: Bondi rescinds policy banning some DOJ employees from attending partisan events
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi has rescinded a policy that prohibited political appointees at the Justice Department from attending campaign events or fun...
